message:
test(golden): fix golden_robot_json flakiness + intentional introspect drift

R2 lens D investigation surfaced two silently-broken golden tests that
had been failing on every clean-env run.

## Health golden: flaky sampler counters (test-oracle quality fix)

`health.json.golden` was captured on a host where the responsiveness
watchdog sampler had already fired one tick (healthy_streak=1,
ticks_total=1, last_snapshot populated, etc.). Fresh test runs race the
sampler timer — often capture before the first tick fires
(healthy_streak=0, last_snapshot=null), which failed the byte-exact
comparison.

Prior scrubber only masked the float VALUES inside last_snapshot
(load_per_core, psi_cpu_some_avg10 → [LIVE_METRIC]). It did NOT
normalize:
  - integer counters (healthy_streak, ticks_total, load_window_len,
    psi_window_len, observations_total) — these tick with each sample
  - last_snapshot and last_reason — null-vs-populated is a timing race

Two new scrub rules:
  * rule 7: numeric counter fields → "[LIVE_COUNTER]"
  * rule 8: last_snapshot (null or object) + last_reason (null or str)
           → "[LIVE_SAMPLE]"
Golden regenerated under the new rules so it locks shape without
racing the sampler. Re-ran and confirmed stable.

## Introspect golden: intentional --refresh flag drift

`introspect.json.golden` missed the --refresh / --catch-up flags
recently added to `cass search` and `cass tui` (commits 3e4e6ef4 +
e570423b). The golden framework correctly caught this as a contract
change. Regenerated to reflect the intentional new CLI surface; diff
is ~23 lines of flag schema additions.

Verified: `cargo test --test golden_robot_json` now passes 7/7 clean
on repeated runs.

The golden framework is doing its job — this commit just catches up
the frozen snapshots to the code's current contract and tightens the
scrubber against a sampler-timing race that was letting the health
golden be silently broken until now.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -162,6 +162,17 @@
           "arg_type": "option",
           "value_type": "path",
           "required": false
+        },
+        {
+          "name": "refresh",
+          "description": "Run an incremental `cass index` pass before launching the TUI so new conversations created since the last index are searchable. No-op when the index is already current; indexing errors are logged and the TUI opens on the existing index (non-fatal)",
+          "arg_type": "flag",
+          "required": false,
+          "default": "false",
+          "enum_values": [
+            "true",
+            "false"
+          ]
         }
       ],
       "has_json_output": false
